data.content: The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. This is a classic MSBuild issue: **globs expand at evaluation time**, before targets run, so build-generated files are invisible to compilation unless explicitly added to the `Compile` item group.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs before `CoreCompile` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before any targets run), so a file created during the build won't be picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, so the compiler knows about it. Also, registering it with `<FileWrites>` ensures the `Clean` target can remove it.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since it's specifically about this scenario.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild globs (implicit `<Compile Include="**/*.cs">`) expand at **evaluation time**, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added two lines inside the target's `<ItemGroup>`:
- `<Compile Include="...">` — explicitly adds the generated file to the compilation inputs
- `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it properly
